# testdata/cohorts

Deterministic `.pulse` fixtures used by Prism tests.

## Files

| File              | Purpose                                                                       |
| ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `tiny.pulse`      | 1000-row demo cohort with 3 fields (`brand_id`, `score`, `age`).              |
| `tiny.synth.json` | Synth spec describing `tiny.pulse`. Used by the generator below.              |

## Regeneration

The fixtures are generated by `internal/devtools/gen-fixtures` with a
hard-coded seed (42). Re-runs produce byte-identical output, so any git
diff in this directory reflects a real spec or seed change.

To regenerate from the repo root:

```bash
go run ./internal/devtools/gen-fixtures
```

The generator writes every fixture listed in `gen-fixtures/main.go::fixtures`.
Add new fixtures by appending an entry there plus a matching `*.synth.json` here.

## Why a separate generator instead of `go generate`?

`go generate` would re-run on every developer workstation and could
silently shadow upstream fixture changes. Keeping the regen behind an
explicit command makes drift visible: if the committed `tiny.pulse`
disagrees with `gen-fixtures` output, the test reproducer prints the
exact CLI to rerun.
